questions.question: The Panchayat Extension to Scheduled Areas Act of 1969 was enacted for the welfare of the people living in which areas?
questions.explanation: For the Scheduled Tribes.

questions.question: The West Bengal Panchayat Act, 1973, provides for the _______________________ of Panchayats at the village level.
questions.explanation: Direct Election.

questions.question: The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendments were enacted to give constitutional status to which of the following institutions?
questions.explanation: Panchayats.

questions.question: The West Bengal Legislative Assembly has _______________________ members.
questions.explanation: 90 members.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Fundamental Duty under the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: To respect the Constitution.

questions.question: Who led the historic Champaran Satyagraha in Bihar which had a significant impact on Bengal?
questions.explanation: It was a major milestone in the Indian Freedom Struggle.

questions.question: Which of the following events took place in Bengal during the Indian Freedom Struggle?
options: The Simon Commission's visit to India
questions.explanation: It was a highly unpopular and divisive move.

questions.question: Who was the president of the Indian National Congress in 1928, which saw significant contributions from Bengal?
questions.explanation: He played a key role in shaping the Congress's future course.

questions.question: What was the name of the key event where Bengal's revolutionaries hatched a plan to assassinate Lord Hardinge?
questions.explanation: It was a significant attempt to avenge the Black Act.

questions.question: Which of the following Bengali personalities was a key figure in the Swadeshi Movement?
questions.explanation: He played a crucial role in organizing the movement.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers forms the international border between India and Bangladesh, passing through West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Ganges River forms the international border.

questions.question: Which of the following mountain ranges is located in the northern part of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Himalayan Range is located in the northern part of West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following cities is the largest in the Dooars region of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Siliguri is the largest city in the Dooars region.

questions.question: Which of the following is a deltaic region in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Bengal Deltas is a deltaic region in West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers forms the Sundarbans delta in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra River forms the Sundarbans delta.

questions.question: The West Bengal government launched 'Duare Duare Paschim Bengal' to provide what services to the citizens?
questions.explanation: Duare Duare Paschim Bengal is a scheme for social welfare services.

questions.question: The 'Mukhyamantri Gram Sadak Yojana' is a scheme launched by the West Bengal government for the development of which infrastructure?
questions.explanation: Mukhyamantri Gram Sadak Yojana is a rural road development scheme.

questions.question: The 'Swasthya Bhita' initiative was launched by the West Bengal government as a part of the?
options: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana
questions.explanation: Swasthya Bhita is a part of the Ayushman Bharat-Health and Wellness Centre initiative.

questions.question: The 'Kanyashree Prakalpa' is a scheme launched by the West Bengal government to empower which group of women?
questions.explanation: Kanyashree Prakalpa is a scheme for unmarried women.

questions.question: The 'Swasthya Bhavan' is a hospital building constructed by the West Bengal government under which scheme?
questions.explanation: Swasthya Bhavan is a part of the Mukhyamantri Swasthya Seva Yojana.

questions.question: A merchant in Kolkata sells an article at a profit of 25%. If he buys it for ₹ 240, what is the selling price of the article?
questions.explanation: The article was bought for ₹ 240 and sold at a 25% profit.

questions.question: A certain quantity of sugar costs ₹ 120 and the cost of 5% more sugar is ₹ 132. What is the percentage increase in cost?
questions.explanation: The cost of 5% more sugar is ₹ 132.

questions.question: A man buys 20 kg of rice at ₹ 18 per kg and sells it at a 20% loss. What is the selling price of the rice per kg?
questions.explanation: The rice was bought at ₹ 18 per kg and sold at a 20% loss.

questions.question: A local merchant in Howrah sells a product at a discount of 15%. If the original price of the product is ₹ 900, what is the selling price?
questions.explanation: The product was sold at a 15% discount.

questions.question: A certain quantity of tea is bought at ₹ 60 per kg and then sold at a 10% profit. What is the selling price of the tea per kg?
questions.explanation: The tea was bought at ₹ 60 per kg and sold at a 10% profit.

questions.question: A person deposits ₹ 10,000 for 2 years at 6% per annum compound interest rate. What will be the amount at the end of 2 years?
questions.explanation: Using compound interest formula.

questions.question: A local cooperative society lends ₹ 20,000 to a farmer at 8% per annum simple interest for 3 years. What will be the interest earned?
questions.explanation: Using simple interest formula.

questions.question: A person deposits ₹ 50,000 for 5 years at 5% per annum compound interest rate. What will be the amount at the end of 5 years?
questions.explanation: Using compound interest formula.

questions.question: A savings bank offers a rate of 5% per annum compound interest. If ₹ 5,000 is deposited for 1 year, what will be the interest earned?
questions.explanation: Using compound interest formula.

questions.question: A person borrows ₹ 30,000 from a bank at 10% per annum simple interest for 2 years. What will be the monthly installment of the simple interest?
questions.explanation: Using simple interest formula.

questions.question: A person can walk at 4 km/h and a person can run at 12 km/h. If they together cover a distance of 5 km in 2 hours, what fraction of the total distance is covered by the person walking?
questions.explanation: The person walking covers 2/3 of the total distance.

questions.question: Two pipes A and B can fill a tank in 6 hours and 8 hours respectively. If both pipes are opened together, how many hours will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: The combined rate of both pipes is 1/4.8 per hour.

questions.question: A man can do a piece of work in 10 days. His wife can do the same work in 15 days. How many days will they take to do the work together?
questions.explanation: Their combined rate is 1/9 per day.

questions.question: Three taps A, B and C can fill a tank in 12, 16 and 24 hours respectively. If tap A is used alone for 8 hours and then tap B and C are opened together, how many more hours will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Tap A fills 1/12 of the tank in 8 hours, and the combined rate of B and C is 1/24 per hour.

questions.question: A group of 6 men can finish a job in 6 days. If 2 more men join them after 2 days, how many more days will it take to finish the job?
questions.explanation: The initial rate of 8 men is 1/6 per day.

questions.question: A bus travels from Kolkata to Asansol at an average speed of 60 km/h. How long will it take to cover the distance of 120 km?
questions.explanation: Time is calculated by dividing the distance by the speed.

questions.question: A train starts from Sealdah station and travels at a speed of 40 km/h. If it takes 3.5 hours to reach a certain station, what distance does it cover?
questions.explanation: Distance is calculated by multiplying the speed by the time.

questions.question: A car travels from Siliguri to Gangtok at an average speed of 80 km/h. If it takes 5 hours to cover the distance, what is the distance covered?
questions.explanation: Distance is calculated by multiplying the speed by the time.

questions.question: A bus travels from Durgapur to Raniganj at an average speed of 50 km/h. If it takes 6 hours to cover the distance, what is the distance covered?
questions.explanation: Distance is calculated by multiplying the speed by the time.

questions.question: A train travels from Howrah to Bardhaman at an average speed of 60 km/h. If the distance between the two stations is 70 km, how much time will it take to cover the distance?
questions.explanation: Time is calculated by dividing the distance by the speed.

questions.question: In the given series 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, 17, the next number would be
questions.explanation: Numbers are increasing by a fixed interval.

questions.question: If DAD is coded as BCB, then how is CAT coded?
questions.explanation: Each letter in the word is shifted two positions forward in the alphabet.

questions.question: What comes next in the sequence of numbers: 1, 2, 4, 7, 11, ___?
questions.explanation: The differences between the numbers are increasing by 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, etc.

questions.question: A particular code translates the word 'RABINDRANATH' into 'YBZLPHWUEJ'. What does the code substitute the letter 'R' with?
questions.explanation: Each vowel is substituted with the letter following it in the alphabet.

questions.question: In a certain code, the word 'MIDNAPORE' is written as 'EPDNHQRW'. How is the letter 'P' coded?
questions.explanation: Each letter is substituted with its position in the alphabet.

questions.question: Amit's father's wife is the sister of Kunal's mother. How is Amit related to Kunal?
questions.explanation: Amit is the son of Kunal's maternal uncle.

questions.question: Sourav's mother's only brother is the father of Rakesh. How is Sourav related to Rakesh?
questions.explanation: Sourav is the brother of Rakesh.

questions.question: Tapan's daughter's son is the son of Tapan's son's wife. What is the relation of Tapan's daughter to Tapan?
questions.explanation: Tapan's daughter is the daughter of Tapan.

questions.question: Rahul's mother is the daughter of Sarojini Devi's mother. How is Rahul related to Sarojini Devi?
questions.explanation: Rahul is the aunt of Sarojini Devi.

questions.question: Sudipto's father's father is the son of Pratibha Devi's mother. What is the relation of Sudipto's father to Pratibha Devi?
questions.explanation: Sudipto's father is the son of Pratibha Devi.

questions.question: If you are standing at the foot of the iconic Victoria Memorial in Kolkata and facing the monument, in which direction will you find the Howrah Bridge?
questions.explanation: You will face the Ganges River.

questions.question: From Darjeeling's Mall Road, if you walk towards the Chaurasta and then take a right turn, in which direction will you be heading?
questions.explanation: You will be heading towards Siliguri.

questions.question: If you are at the Birla Mandir in Kolkata and you walk towards the Hooghly River, in which direction will you be facing?
questions.explanation: You will be facing the river.

questions.question: A person starts walking from the Park Circus area of Kolkata and takes a left turn after 1 km. Then he walks for another 1 km. In which direction will he be walking?
questions.explanation: He will be walking north.

questions.question: From the Dakshineswar Kali Temple, if you walk towards the Ganges River and then take a right turn, in which direction will you be heading?
questions.explanation: You will be heading towards Belur Math.
